arts.ttl: Chelsea could have easier route to sign Newcastle target – BlueCo ‘key’ for summer move
arts.sum: Chelsea and Newcastle United are in the picture to sign RC Strasbourg’s Emanuel Emegha in the summer window. Journalist Mohamed Toubache-Ter took to X, formerly known as Twitter, to state Chelsea have the 22-year-old’s name is ‘on the table’. The striker’s ‘meteoric rise’ at the Ligue 1 club has caught Newcastle’s attention. arts.ttl: Is Newcastle United vs Brentford on TV? How to follow, live stream details and kick-off time
arts.sum: Even though Newcastle vs Brentford is not being televised in the UK, the match is still available for Magpies' fans to watch around the globe. Newcastle's clash against Brentford is scheduled to kick-off at 7.45pm on Wednesday, April 2 at St James' Park.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United hear Chris Rigg transfer message as Man United eye 'cheaper' Sunderland deal
arts.sum: “If you’ve got talented players and they’re playing every single week in the Championship and they’re continuously getting better, then the Premier League clubs are going to have a look and try and sign them now because they’re going to get them for cheaper. He believes Rigg is a'superstar' in the making and knows that Premier League clubs will feel they can get the player for a bargain fee while he's still playing in the Championship.

arts.ttl: Eddie Howe confirms Newcastle United are close to deal for super defender
arts.sum: Newcastle United manager Eddie Howe has confirmed that Fabian Schar should be set to sign his new contract with the club imminently. The veteran Swiss centre-back has been a key player at St James’ Park for several years now, and it seems he’s set to extend his stay for a bit longer. Schar still has […] The post Eddie Howe confirms Newcastle United are close to deal for super defender appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Bryan Mbeumo ready for 'ideal' Newcastle United transfer as Paul Mitchell told huge asking price
arts.sum: Reports in January claimed Brentford were asking for £75m for Mbeumo but Blackham feels United could get the 25-year-old for less even if he thinks his worth to the club is close to record amounts. United would face fierce competition for Mbeumo's signature with Liverpool also credited with interest - but a move to St James' Park has been tipped as the right one by journalist and Brentford fan Daniel Blackham.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United learn £700m truth as Tottenham chief Daniel Levy makes stadium statement
arts.sum: Whether Newcastle decide to transform St James' Park or to build a new ground not too far away, clearly, the stadium is going to be crucial to the club's prospects of turbocharging revenues in a PSR world - just as it has for Spurs as chairman Daniel Levy explained. Spurs also generated £255.2m in commercial revenue, including third-party events, visitor attractions and conferences, with the help of the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium whereas Newcastle raised less than a third of that figure (£83.6m) despite huge growth.

arts.ttl: Newcastle vs Brentford – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Newcastle United return to Premier League action tonight when Brentford visit St James’ Park. Eddie Howe’s side celebrated Carabao Cup success before the international break, as the Magpies beat Liverpool at Wembley. Goals from Dan Burn and Alexander Isak sealed a 2-1 win for Newcastle. That win ended the club’s 70-year wait for a major […] The post Newcastle vs Brentford – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: One Newcastle United star still has a lot to prove despite Wembley heroics
arts.sum: One more needed to bring up 20 Premier League goals in successive seasons, something never achieved by any United striker. Definition of Isak according to the Oxford English Dictionary: brings goals to Newcastle.

arts.ttl: Strong push, Chelsea fear, dark horse - Newcastle United season finish predicted
arts.sum: Newcastle United's Carabao Cup final and the celebrations that followed can now be put to one side as focus turns to securing a Champions League finish to crown off a season for the ages. We therefore asked our Chronicle Live team to predict the remaining fixtures of the season to determine whether Newcastle make it into the Champions League.
